channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police charge 16-year-old with murder in death of girl in Austin
item.description: Austin Police said a 16-year-old boy was arrested in connection with the Monday death of a 16-year-old girl. The boy has been charged with murder.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard will seek to arrest political opponents as she faces eviction
item.description: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard has suggested she'll seek to arrest political opponents, many of whom have raised concerns about her lavish spending on the taxpayers dime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida sues Garland for blocking Trump assassination attempt investigation
item.description: Florida sued Attorney General Merrick Garland on Wednesday, saying the Justice Department is improperly blocking the state from investigating the second assassination attempt of former President Trump. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia's Gullah-Geechee community seeks path forward after deadly dock collapse
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — The partial collapse of a ferry dock that claimed the lives of seven people attending a cultural festival has left a historic Black community in Georgia struggling with how to move forward.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey struck suspected Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day on Thursday following an attack on the premises of a key defense company that killed at least five people, the state-run news agency reported.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The 3 men convicted of murdering Ahmaud Arbery motion for new trial
item.description: Travis and Gregory McMichael armed themselves with guns and jumped in a pickup truck to chase Arbery, a 25-year-old Black man, after they saw him run past their house on Feb. 23, 2020.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, KREX 5

item.title: DNC hits Trump over reported praise of Hitler's generals in mobile billboard
item.description: The Democratic National Committee (DNC) is deploying a mobile billboard ahead of former President Donald Trump’s rally in Las Vegas on Thursday, hitting him over his past reported praise of Adolf Hitler’s generals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: The EPA imposes stricter standards to protect children from exposure to lead paint
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Two weeks after setting a nationwide deadline for removal of lead pipes, the Biden administration is imposing strict new limits on dust from lead-based paint in older homes and child-care facilities.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harris says Trump 'is a fascist' after his ex-chief of staff says he wanted generals like Hitler's
item.description: ASTON, Pa. (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris said that she believes that Donald Trump "is a fascist” after his longest-serving chief of staff said the former president praised Adolf Hitler while in office and put personal loyalty above the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: DAN MERICA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Quarter of young Black men say they will vote for Trump: Survey
item.description: Despite one-fourth of young Black men saying they would cast their ballot for Trump, they repeatedly identify Harris as the strongest candidate on the issues.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina lawmakers convene again to address Hurricane Helene's billions in damages
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina state legislators returning to work Thursday to consider further Hurricane Helene relief have received an estimate of the monetary scope of the catastrophic flooding and what Gov. Roy Cooper wants them to spend soon on recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ron Ely, TV's 'Tarzan' in the 1960s, dies at 86
item.description: Ron Ely, the tall, musclebound actor who played the title character in the 1960s NBC series “Tarzan,” has died at age 86.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Before Taylor Swift show in New Orleans, a homeless encampment is forced to move
item.description: In anticipation of three Taylor Swift concerts, which could draw around 150,000 visitors to New Orleans' Superdome this weekend, state authorities on Wednesday began clearing a nearby homeless encampment.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida mailman stabs lawncare worker over parking: deputies
item.description: The Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office has released more information about the events that may have led up to a deadly stabbing Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about E. coli and the McDonald's outbreak
item.description: A food poisoning outbreak tied to McDonald’s Quarter Pounders was caused by a common type of E. coli bacteria that can cause serious illness and death. Particularly vulnerable are young children, older people or those with weakened immune systems.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TV host Adam Richman says production crew robbed while filming in North London
item.description: TV host Adam Richman, best known for starring in "Man v. Food," says he and his production crew were robbed while filming a show in the UK.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Quarter Pounders are off the menu at 20% of McDonald's in US as E. coli cases are investigated
item.description: McDonald’s worked Wednesday to reassure customers that its U.S. restaurants are safe as federal investigators tried to pinpoint the cause of a deadly E. coli outbreak linked to the fast-food giant's Quarter Pounder hamburgers. McDonald's pulled Quarter Pounders from one-fifth of its U.S.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A man who threatened to kill Democratic election officials pleads guilty
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A Colorado man repeatedly made online threats about killing the top elections officials in his state and Arizona — both Democrats — as well as a judge and law enforcement agents, according to a guilty plea he entered Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bad state data may misdirect nearly $1 billion in federal funds to replace lead pipes
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — The Environmental Protection Agency is at risk of misallocating nearly $1 billion in lead pipe replacement funding to the wrong states because it didn’t verify inaccurate data provided by Texas and Florida, an agency watchdog announced.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pilot Chuck Coleman killed in air show crash in Las Cruces
item.description: Coleman was an aerobatic flight instructor for actors in the 2022 motion picture, "Top Gun: Maverick," where he flew more than 100 flights preparing actors to fly in Navy F-18 Hornets.
item.dc:creator: Dave Burge, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 dogs die from starvation, North Carolina man charged with animal cruelty
item.description: At the scene, police found the dog in question dead alongside another dead dog inside a small, enclosed fence with three other dogs.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, NewsNation

item.title: DNA links a suspected Chicago-area serial killer who died in 1981 to a woman's 1979 death
item.description: NORTH AURORA, Ill. (AP) — DNA found on the clothing of a suburban Chicago woman who was kidnapped and killed 45 years ago matches that of a suspected serial killer who died two years later, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Colorado girl, 8, fights off attacker wearing clown mask in her bedroom
item.description: A Colorado man is accused of assaulting an 8-year-old girl in her bedroom but fled when she fought back, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video: Suspected drunken driver passes within feet of Harris' motorcade in Wisconsin
item.description: The driver was traveling in the wrong direction on the interstate in Milwaukee when deputies spotted him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump praised 'Hitler's generals': former chief of staff says
item.description: Retired Gen. John Kelly, who served as White House chief of staff in the Trump administration, said former President Trump praised Adolf Hitler's generals for their loyalty in a new interview in The Atlantic on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KTLA 5

item.title: Suspect in shootings of Democratic office in Arizona had over 120 guns in his home, officials find
item.description: An Arizona prosecutor says the man arrested in the three-time shooting of a Democratic campaign office had more than 120 guns and over 250,000 rounds of ammunition in his home.
item.dc:creator: Anita Snow And Walter Berry, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Abercrombie & Fitch chief Mike Jeffries arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Mike Jeffries, his romantic partner and a third man were arrested Tuesday on charges of luring men into drug-laced, outlandish and coercive sex parties by dangling the promise of modeling for the retailer's once-defining beefcake ads.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina county surrounding Asheville overcounted Helene deaths by as many as 30, sheriff says
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The North Carolina county that is home to Asheville overcounted deaths caused by Helene by as many as 30, according to a statement Tuesday from its sheriff’s office and data from the state, significantly reducing the death toll from the historic storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: McDonald's Quarter Pounders linked to deadly E. coli outbreak
item.description: McDonald's Quarter Pounders sold at restaurants across the country may have been contaminated with E. coli, according to a warning from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. The CDC says at least 49 people have contracted E.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden heaps praise on Slovenia PM for aiding release of Americans in major US-Russia prisoner swap
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden heaped praise on Slovenian Prime Minister Robert Golob on Tuesday for his role in the recent seven-nation prisoner swap that freed Americans Evan Gershkovich, Alsu Kurmasheva and Paul Whelan from Russian prison. The deal, completed in August, was the largest U.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hurricane Oscar forms in the Caribbean
item.description: The formation of Oscar and Nadine comes as parts of the U.S. have been hit hard by a pair of hurricanes, Helene and Milton, that devastated communities, destroyed roads, caused power outages, took lives and injured many.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: LIST: Waffles, pancakes recall expanded over potential listeria contamination
item.description: The recalled products were sold under multiple brand names across the entire country, the company says.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5
